summary: Complete testing of Interdiction Planning (QED Snare) and Chase Calculator features on Route Planner. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests passed for /api/routes/interdiction and /api/routes/chase endpoints. Frontend: All Route Planner features verified working including 3-tab layout (Route/Interdiction/Chase), snare circle visualization on map, route lines, and verdict text display.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/routes/interdiction single origin full coverage (100%) - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/routes/interdiction multiple origins (hurston, arccorp, crusader → microtech) - PASSED
Backend: Snare range affects coverage (2 Mkm vs 15 Mkm) - PASSED
Backend: Interdiction invalid destination returns 400 error - PASSED
Backend: Interdiction no valid origins returns 400 error - PASSED
Backend: Interdiction empty origins returns 400 error - PASSED
Backend: Interdiction returns snare_position with distance_to_dest_mkm - PASSED
Backend: Interdiction cross-system routes use gateway entry point - PASSED

Backend: POST /api/routes/chase S1 vs S2 (slower) can_catch=false - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/routes/chase same QD size can_catch=false - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/routes/chase S3 vs S1 maximum speed advantage - PASSED
Backend: Chase prep_time_seconds affects total_time_seconds - PASSED
Backend: Chase distance_mkm affects closing_time_seconds - PASSED
Backend: Chase verdict text contains speed info - PASSED
Backend: Chase all QD sizes (1, 2, 3) return correct speeds - PASSED
Frontend: Route Planner /routes page loads with 3 tabs (Route, Interdiction, Chase) - PASSED
Frontend: Tab switching between Route/Interdiction/Chase works correctly - PASSED
Frontend: Route tab shows origin/destination selectors and QD size buttons - PASSED
Frontend: Route calculation displays distance (53.16 Mkm), time (4m 48s), waypoints - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction tab shows QED Snare Planning panel - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction destination selector works (microTech selected) - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction add multiple origins (Hurston, ArcCorp, Crusader) - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction snare range slider adjustable - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction results show 100% coverage, 3/3 routes covered - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction map shows red snare circle with SNARE label - PASSED
Frontend: Interdiction map shows dashed route lines from origins to destination - PASSED
Frontend: Chase tab shows QD size buttons for you and target - PASSED
Frontend: Chase S2 vs S1 shows 'Target Catchable' with time estimate - PASSED
Frontend: Chase S1 vs S2 shows 'Cannot Catch' with speed disadvantage - PASSED

Frontend: Chase results show Your QD speed, Target QD speed, closing time, total time - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: Interdiction algorithm correctly calculates optimal snare position by sampling 200 points along approach corridor
Cross-system interdiction properly uses gateway entry points for route calculation
Chase calculator correctly implements speed comparison (S1: 165k, S2: 190k, S3: 240k km/s)
Frontend properly renders snare circle and route lines on canvas map
Tab switching correctly shows/hides appropriate panels
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - interdiction and chase use static star_systems.py data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Interdiction and Chase features fully tested and working. The /api/routes/interdiction endpoint accepts origins array and destination, returns optimal snare position, coverage_pct, routes_covered, route_lines. The /api/routes/chase endpoint compares QD speeds and returns can_catch, verdict, speeds, closing_time. Frontend displays snare circle on map with route lines for interdiction, and verdict text with speed comparison for chase. QD speeds: S1=165k, S2=190k, S3=240k km/s.

feature_verification_summary.interdiction_api: PASS - POST /api/routes/interdiction returns snare_position, coverage_pct, route_lines
feature_verification_summary.interdiction_multi_origin: PASS - Accepts multiple origins, calculates optimal snare for all routes
feature_verification_summary.interdiction_snare_range: PASS - Adjustable range affects coverage percentage
feature_verification_summary.interdiction_cross_system: PASS - Uses gateway entry points for cross-system origins
feature_verification_summary.interdiction_map_visualization: PASS - Red snare circle and dashed route lines visible
feature_verification_summary.chase_api: PASS - POST /api/routes/chase returns can_catch, speeds, verdict
feature_verification_summary.chase_faster_catches: PASS - S2 vs S1 shows catchable with time estimate
feature_verification_summary.chase_slower_cannot: PASS - S1 vs S2 shows cannot catch, suggests interdiction
feature_verification_summary.chase_same_speed: PASS - Same QD shows cannot close gap
feature_verification_summary.chase_verdict_text: PASS - Verdict includes speed info and recommendation
feature_verification_summary.route_tab: PASS - Standard route planning still works (tested Hurston→microTech)

response.verdict: Target's QD is 25,000 km/s faster. You cannot catch them in quantum. Use interdiction instead.
